content:
cascade_analysis.py attributes FR error to BLEND or DRIVE by comparing captures that differ in that one knob's VALUE, but in this 12-capture matrix no two captures actually share every OTHER knob setting too (verified 2026-07-16: printed every capture's full settings dict, no duplicates outside blend). So every "BL=0.65 excess vs BL=1.00 baseline" or "swing across DRIVE" number is really (blend/drive effect) + (difference from whatever else also changed between those two captures — presence, level, bass, treble, mid, etc).

This is not fixable with the current captures (see ISS-008's "no capture-based isolation is possible" note — no BLEND=0 file, no matched-knob pairs to difference algebraically) and no trustworthy new captures are available either.

When this matters vs when it doesn't:
- SAFE to trust: effects far larger than any plausible single-knob contribution, monotonic across multiple settings, isolated to one revision. Example: ISS-008's V2 dry-path HF excess (+8.7 -> +11.2 -> +15.3dB monotonic across 3 different BL values, up to +54dB at the extreme) — no tone/presence knob swing explains that shape or magnitude, so the BLEND attribution is trustworthy despite the confound.
- WEAK, do not treat as settled: smaller effects (a few dB), especially where BASS/TREBLE/PRESENCE differ substantially between the compared captures. Example: ISS-005's V2 BASS-hump correlation with MID-shift throw — flagged in that issue as needing re-diagnosis for exactly this reason.

Any future issue built on cascade_analysis.py output should state explicitly whether it clears this "safe to trust" bar (large + monotonic + revision-isolated) or not, and treat "not" cases as a hypothesis, not a finding.

The clean way out of this confound (short of new captures) is the SPICE-target cross-check (docs/reference-fr-targets.md) — those targets are per-stage, single-control, and already isolate what real captures here cannot. See the companion note on that.
title: Methodology caveat: cascade_analysis.py's blend/drive attribution is confounded by multi-knob captures
